acceptable_sentence: The committee broke off the negotiations.
unacceptable_sentence: The committee broke off the agenda.
expert_comment: Agenda is a plausible committee noun, but broke off the agenda is mainly a collocational anomaly.

acceptable_sentence: The officer handed over the documents.
unacceptable_sentence: The officer handed over the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is not transferable; mismatch is local, but a more task-plausible object is needed.

acceptable_sentence: The old engine gave out suddenly.
unacceptable_sentence: The old answer gave out suddenly.
expert_comment: The old answer gave out is rejected from subject oddness, not specifically PV knowledge.

acceptable_sentence: She put in steady effort yesterday.
unacceptable_sentence: She put in steady evidence yesterday.
expert_comment: Put in evidence can be acceptable in legal/register contexts, so the mismatch is not controlled.

acceptable_sentence: The lecture was hard to take in.
unacceptable_sentence: The doorway was hard to take in.
expert_comment: Doorway is rejected by broad object semantics; take in is also polysemous.

acceptable_sentence: The mayor sat back during the crisis.
unacceptable_sentence: The mayor sat back during the sprint.
expert_comment: Runner/sprint creates a world-knowledge mismatch; sit back is also posture/polysemy sensitive.

acceptable_sentence: Poor training held back the players.
unacceptable_sentence: Extra support held back the players.
expert_comment: Extra support can sometimes hold players back, so the intended mismatch is unstable.

acceptable_sentence: She moved up to senior manager.
unacceptable_sentence: She moved up to junior assistant.
expert_comment: The contrast is hierarchy-based and locally tied to move up; this is one of the stronger items.

acceptable_sentence: The tourists got on the bus.
unacceptable_sentence: The tourists got on the lecture.
expert_comment: Lecture is not a vehicle; the item is too easily solved from object category.

acceptable_sentence: A rare chance came along today.
unacceptable_sentence: A rare schedule came along today.
expert_comment: A schedule can come along in informal usage; the mismatch is weak and unstable.

acceptable_sentence: Her analysis stood out among reports.
unacceptable_sentence: Her analysis stood out among deadlines.
expert_comment: Among deadlines is an odd comparison set; use a more parallel competing-item set.

acceptable_sentence: The company pulled back from investment.
unacceptable_sentence: The company pulled back from approval.
expert_comment: Pulled back from approval is locally wrong, but approval is not a clean activity/involvement noun.

acceptable_sentence: The office ran out of paper.
unacceptable_sentence: The office ran out of agenda.
expert_comment: Agenda makes the depletion mismatch too obvious from noun semantics alone.

acceptable_sentence: The officer turned over the files.
unacceptable_sentence: The officer turned over the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is too generic; use a better legal/authority near-miss.

acceptable_sentence: The school put on a concert.
unacceptable_sentence: The school put on a textbook.
expert_comment: Textbook is not an event; mismatch is clear but solved by noun type.

acceptable_sentence: The party took back the council.
unacceptable_sentence: The party took back the outcome.
expert_comment: Took back the council is less natural than took back control; outcome is a poor near-miss.

acceptable_sentence: I could barely make out the sign outside.
unacceptable_sentence: I could barely make out the flavor outside.
expert_comment: Make out the flavor is actually acceptable as discern a flavor; item cannot be used as keyed.

acceptable_sentence: The hard work paid off eventually.
unacceptable_sentence: The hard weather paid off eventually.
expert_comment: Weather subject makes the item solvable without PV knowledge.

acceptable_sentence: The children got off the train.
unacceptable_sentence: The children got off the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is not a vehicle/location; use a travel-context near-miss.

acceptable_sentence: He turned up at the meeting.
unacceptable_sentence: He turned up at the method.
expert_comment: Method is not a place/event; mismatch is broad object semantics.

acceptable_sentence: The soldiers got down behind the wall.
unacceptable_sentence: The soldiers got down behind the idea.
expert_comment: Idea is not a physical location; this is a general concreteness cue.

acceptable_sentence: The host cut off the speaker.
unacceptable_sentence: The host cut off the policy.
expert_comment: Cut off the policy targets object type rather than the interrupt-someone sense.

acceptable_sentence: The firm brought in an expert.
unacceptable_sentence: The firm brought in a deadline.
expert_comment: Bring in a deadline can mean introduce a deadline, so the keyed response is unsafe.

acceptable_sentence: Police put out a warning.
unacceptable_sentence: Police put out a wallet.
expert_comment: Wallet is too object-like; put out also has literal object-placement senses.

acceptable_sentence: The editor cleaned up the insults.
unacceptable_sentence: The editor cleaned up the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is simply not cleanable; broad noun semantics solves the item.

acceptable_sentence: The report set out the rules.
unacceptable_sentence: The report set out the pencils.
expert_comment: Report plus pencils is a subject-object mismatch; set out has literal arrangement senses.

acceptable_sentence: The teacher broke down the problem.
unacceptable_sentence: The teacher broke down the applause.
expert_comment: Applause is odd but the mismatch is fairly local to the decompose-an-object sense.

acceptable_sentence: The manager reached out to clients.
unacceptable_sentence: The manager reached out to deadlines.
expert_comment: Deadlines are not recipients; a human/institutional near-miss would be better.

acceptable_sentence: She kept up the good work.
unacceptable_sentence: She kept up the heavy claim.
expert_comment: Heavy claim is unnatural independent of keep up.

acceptable_sentence: They put up posters downtown.
unacceptable_sentence: They put up music downtown.
expert_comment: Music is less attachable/displayable than posters, but the item may be too noun-category based.

acceptable_sentence: The coach turned around the team.
unacceptable_sentence: The coach turned around the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is a weak object for business improvement; use a more plausible but wrong object.

acceptable_sentence: The data backed up the claim.
unacceptable_sentence: The data backed up the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is not a proposition; mismatch is local but still object-type based.

acceptable_sentence: She looked out at the garden.
unacceptable_sentence: She looked out at the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is not visually look-out-at material; broad noun semantics solves it.

acceptable_sentence: He took out a loan yesterday.
unacceptable_sentence: He took out a deadline yesterday.
expert_comment: Deadline is not an official service/document; object-type cue remains strong.

acceptable_sentence: They looked back on the decision.
unacceptable_sentence: They looked back on the ceiling.
expert_comment: Ceiling creates a broad object mismatch; not a good past-event near-miss.

acceptable_sentence: The rent went up sharply.
unacceptable_sentence: The advice went up sharply.
expert_comment: Advice can change in quality/amount metaphorically; the key is ambiguous.

acceptable_sentence: The rent went down sharply.
unacceptable_sentence: The advice went down sharply.
expert_comment: Advice went down has competing idioms and is not a stable unacceptable item.

acceptable_sentence: Oh, come on, that sounds impossible.
unacceptable_sentence: Oh, come on, that sounds accurate.
expert_comment: Come on is discourse/prosody sensitive; audio intonation can flip the interpretation.

acceptable_sentence: Experts came in during planning.
unacceptable_sentence: Experts came in during deadline.
expert_comment: During deadline is ungrammatical; item fails surface naturalness.

acceptable_sentence: The truth came out during interviews.
unacceptable_sentence: The furniture came out during interviews.
expert_comment: Furniture as subject makes the item trivially odd for the reveal sense.

acceptable_sentence: The reforms brought about major change.
unacceptable_sentence: The reforms brought about major furniture.
expert_comment: Furniture is not an event/result; broad noun semantics solves the item.

acceptable_sentence: The researchers carried out the survey.
unacceptable_sentence: The researchers carried out the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is not a task/study; mismatch is local but object-type based.

acceptable_sentence: The evidence ruled out that explanation.
unacceptable_sentence: The evidence ruled out that deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline is not an explanation/possibility; acceptable but could be a better near-miss.

acceptable_sentence: The teacher pointed out the error.
unacceptable_sentence: The pencil pointed out the error.
expert_comment: Pencil subject is trivially non-agentive; not enough PV-meaning dependency.

acceptable_sentence: The paragraph summed up the argument.
unacceptable_sentence: The paragraph summed up the deadline.
expert_comment: Deadline object is a broad object-type cue; use a text/content near-miss.

acceptable_sentence: The nurse followed up after surgery.
unacceptable_sentence: The nurse followed up after furniture.
expert_comment: After furniture is surface-odd and does not test the follow-up sense cleanly.

acceptable_sentence: The chart laid out the plan.
unacceptable_sentence: The chart laid out the spoon.
expert_comment: Chart plus spoon creates a broad object mismatch; lay out is also literal/polysemous.

acceptable_sentence: The students worked out the answer.
unacceptable_sentence: The students worked out the carpet.
expert_comment: Carpet is a broad object mismatch and does not test solution calculation cleanly.
